title: validate.sh dispatches undocumented rule scripts outside the advertised audit surface
evidence: The default get_rule_scripts() branch emits every check-*.sh file plus five injected TS_RULE entries, so AI_PROTOCOLS, COMPLEXITY_MATCH, FOLDER_NAMING, FRONTMATTER_VALID, SECTION_COUNTS, and TEMPLATE_SOURCE still run even though they are outside the packet's 21-rule contract and absent from the help/severity registry.
recommendation: Replace alphabetical directory dispatch with a canonical rule registry that drives help text, severity, and execution from one source.

title: The 21-rule surface mixes template contracts with operational runtime guards, leaving several iter 1 fields structurally unowned
evidence: Seven targeted rules act on merge payloads, save snapshots, repo-wide scans, or generated graph metadata instead of authored template fields, while shared frontmatter fields like title, description, trigger_phrases, importance_tier, contextType, session_dedup.*, completion_pct, open_questions, answered_questions, and HVR_REFERENCE have no dedicated validator in the 21-rule surface.
recommendation: Split reporting into template-structure vs operational/save-time rule families and either add explicit validators for orphan fields or document them as intentionally unchecked.

subject: rule-coverage-remediation
reason: This pass classified coverage and contract drift only; no validator/template edits were made because the review target is read-only for this iteration.
